## 1. Self-check (run BEFORE asking the PO anything)

Same as `docs/session-log/TEMPLATES/handoff-cold-devin.md` §1 — do not skip. Run all four sub-steps (1a `secrets list`, 1b repo clone / pull, 1c `python3 scripts/validate_docs.py`, 1d `gh --version` if needed). Expect §1c to print `validated 81 artifact(s); 0 failed` on the post-TKT-017 main.

If `validate_docs.py` reports failures on a fresh `main` checkout, **stop and tell the PO**. Main was clean as of this snapshot's commit; any failure indicates either an upstream change that landed after this file was written, or your local checkout is out of sync.

## 4. Pipeline (stable — full version in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §3)

```
PRD (Business Planner)
  └→ Reviewer #1 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Planner family)
       └→ ArchSpec + ADRs + Tickets (Architect)
            └→ Reviewer #2 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Architect family)
                 └→ Code (Executor; one Ticket per session; GLM/DeepSeek/Codex)
                      └→ Reviewer #3 (RV-CODE, Kimi K2.6) + Qodo PR-Agent bot
                           └→ Ticket Orchestrator first cross-reviewer audit
                                └→ Devin Orchestrator ratification audit
                                     └→ PO merges
```

The Ticket Orchestrator pass-1 audit is **optional in current practice** — for TKT-017@0.1.0 the PO ran the Executor and Reviewer directly and skipped Ticket Orchestrator dispatch. Devin Orchestrator pass-2 ratification was the only audit gate. This is acceptable; the two-phase audit is codified for future use but not strictly required.

## 5. Roles and write-zones (canonical: `CONTRIBUTING.md` § Roles)

Same role table as `docs/session-log/TEMPLATES/handoff-cold-devin.md` §5. Your write-zone (Devin Orchestrator) covers:

- `docs/session-log/` (this file, future snapshots)
- `docs/backlog/` (light edits; new BACKLOG entries scoped to specific findings)
- Ticket frontmatter promotions (`status` field transitions, `arch_ref`, `version`, `updated`, `completed_at/by/note`)
- Light reference-pinning in ticket / review bodies during clerical closure-PRs (e.g., `TKT-017` → `TKT-017@0.1.0` so `validate_docs.py` passes)
- Closure-PRs after Reviewer verdict + merge of Executor + Review PRs
- Cross-TKT shared-interface conflict resolution
- Ratification audit + final merge-safe sign-off prompts

You **must NOT** write code, formal artifact bodies (PRD/ARCH/ADR/RV substantive content), `docs/prompts/`, or anything outside the write-zone above.

## 6. Current state — 2026-05-05 (post-TKT-017 close)

### Artifact phase

PRD-002@0.2.1 (`docs/prd/PRD-002-observability-and-scale-readiness.md`) is the active observability PRD with G1 (tenant-isolation breach detection), G2 (model-stall detection), G3 (PR-Agent CI tail latency telemetry), and G4 (config-driven allowlist load tests) as its four headline gates. ARCH-001@0.5.0 specifies the four corresponding components (C12 G1 detector, C13 G2 detector, C14 G3 telemetry, C15 G4 load harness). Architect has decomposed those into TKT-017 through TKT-020.

| Ticket | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| TKT-017@0.1.0 | done (this cycle) | C12 G1 BreachDetector + BreachDetectingTenantStore wrapper merged. F-M1 follow-up filed in BACKLOG. |
| TKT-018@0.1.0 | draft | C13 G2 model-stall detector + synthetic tests. Awaiting RV-SPEC Reviewer pass before promotion to `ready`. |
| TKT-019@0.1.0 | draft | C14 G3 PR-Agent CI tail latency telemetry. Awaiting RV-SPEC Reviewer pass. |
| TKT-020@0.1.0 | draft | C15 G4 config-driven allowlist load tests. Awaiting RV-SPEC Reviewer pass. |

PRD-003@0.1.2 (`tracking-modalities-expansion`) exists but is downstream of the PRD-002 G1–G4 closure. Do not dispatch executor work on PRD-003 tickets until PRD-002's gates are all done.

|---|---|---|---|---|
| `<this closure-PR>` | `devin/<timestamp>-tkt-017-closure` | TKT-017@0.1.0 status `done`, RV-CODE-017 clerical pinning fixes (9 unpinned references → pinned), BACKLOG entry for F-M1 production wiring, this session-log snapshot | pending | PO review + merge |

### Last action taken (by previous orchestrator)

This Devin orchestrator (session `fd8207b7`) completed the TKT-017@0.1.0 cycle:

1. Received PR #120 from GLM 5.1 Executor at iter-1 HEAD `ed20f5dd`. Ratification audit (3-layer: scope, redaction, AC mapping) passed.
2. Coordinated Kimi K2.6 RV-CODE-017 review. Iter-1 verdict `pass_with_changes` on F-M1 / F-M2 / F-L1.
3. Triaged findings: F-M1 → BACKLOG; F-M2 + F-L1 → iter-2 corrections to Executor.
4. Iter-2 Executor pass at HEAD `d227b6b` resolved F-L1 (inline `pruneBreachTimestamps`) and F-M2 (JSDoc + RLS-deny test). 693/693 tests passed locally.
5. Iter-2 Kimi verify at rv HEAD `8a3e76e` → verdict `pass`, `status: approved` on RV-CODE-017.
6. Encountered GitHub Actions hosted-runner provisioning flake on iter-2 HEAD: three retries each timed out at 15 minutes in queue with no logs and conclusion=cancelled-promoted-to-failure on both `validate-docs` and `PR Agent` workflows on PR #120 + PR #121. Diagnosed as infrastructure issue, not code defect.
7. Issued merge-safe sign-off with squash-merge order (PR #120 first, then PR #121, branch deletion via GitHub UI) plus exact squash commit titles + descriptions.
8. PO chose option A: merge now with documented infra flake. Both PRs squash-merged to main as `c9a441c4` (executor) and `af0e98e0` (reviewer).
9. Post-merge: validate_docs.py reported 9 unpinned `TKT-017` / `TKT-018` references in the merged RV-CODE-017 file. Applied clerical pinning (Kimi-written body content unchanged in substance; only ticket-id references pinned to `@0.1.0` per the same pattern as the TKT-016 closure clerical fix).
10. Filed BACKLOG entry for F-M1 in `docs/backlog/observability-followups.md` § "TKT-NEW-wire-breach-detector-into-production-boot-path".
11. Promoted TKT-017@0.1.0 frontmatter to `status: done` with `completed_at`, `completed_by`, `completed_note`.
12. Generated this cold-handoff snapshot (auto-cold rule) and packaged the four changes into one closure-PR for PO review.

### Next step

The PO's next pipeline action is to dispatch RV-SPEC review on the next ticket in the PRD-002 G1–G4 sequence. The natural next ticket is **TKT-018@0.1.0** (G2 model-stall detector + synthetic tests). Sequence:

1. **PO** asks Architect (Codex CLI / opencode + GPT-5.5 thinking) to confirm TKT-018 design is final and frontmatter is `status: draft`.
2. **PO** dispatches Kimi K2.6 RV-SPEC Reviewer on TKT-018 + ARCH-001@0.5.0 §3.13 (the C13 component spec). Reviewer writes `docs/reviews/RV-SPEC-NNN-tkt-018-...md`.
3. On Reviewer `pass` or `pass_with_changes`+resolution, **PO** flips TKT-018 frontmatter to `status: ready` (clerical commit; the Devin Orchestrator can do this in a small clerical PR).
4. **PO** dispatches GLM 5.1 Executor on TKT-018. Executor PR arrives → Devin Orchestrator ratifies → Kimi RV-CODE → merge.
5. Repeat for TKT-019 and TKT-020.

### Outstanding decisions / Q-PO items

- **F-M1 BACKLOG entry exists** under `docs/backlog/observability-followups.md` § "TKT-NEW-wire-breach-detector-into-production-boot-path". The PO needs to choose when to convert this BACKLOG entry into a numbered TKT (likely after TKT-018+TKT-019+TKT-020 close, since wiring all four detectors+telemetry components into production boot path may be batched). Architect should ratify the per-request-wrapping vs `TenantStoreFactory` choice when the BACKLOG entry is promoted to a TKT.
- **CI infrastructure flake on 2026-05-05** — three independent retry attempts on both PR #120 (executor) and PR #121 (reviewer) iter-2 HEADs all hit GitHub Actions hosted-runner provisioning timeouts (15-minute queue with no logs, conclusion=cancelled-promoted-to-failure). Same pattern was previously seen on the morning of 2026-05-05 against rv-RV-CODE-016 (~11:40 UTC) and on 2026-05-04 evening against rv-RV-SPEC-010 (~22:36 UTC). If you observe this pattern again, recognise it as runner-pool exhaustion, not code regression — verify locally on the iter-N HEAD that build/lint/typecheck/tests/validate_docs are green, then make a merge-safety call with the PO. Do NOT block on red CI when (a) the prior iter HEAD CI was green, (b) the iter delta is purely additive in semantic-low-risk paths, and (c) the Reviewer (Kimi) approved the new HEAD substantively.
- **GLM 5.1 Executor stall pattern** — on iter-1 of TKT-017 the Executor stalled three consecutive times around the 105K-token / ~52% context point ("thinking out loud" budget exhaustion mid-stream, cut off mid-token like `export const BRE...`). Recovered by issuing a locked-design continuation prompt (no redesign latitude) with a rigid 23-step procedure requiring `git push` after every step. Each step is small enough to commit even if the next attempt stalls. This is a relevant prior for TKT-018 if dispatched to GLM 5.1; the same locked-design + per-step push discipline should be the default ITER-1 procedure for any Executor on a TKT with non-trivial scope.

### Tooling assumptions

- Architect: Codex CLI / opencode + GPT-5.5 thinking on PO's Windows PC
- Executor: opencode + OmniRoute, GLM 5.1 default (DeepSeek V4 Pro fallback if GLM stalls again), Codex GPT-5.5 specialist
- Reviewer: opencode + OmniRoute, Kimi K2.6 (no model deviation in this cycle)
- PR-Agent (auto bot): GitHub Actions, GPT-5.3 Codex via OmniRoute
- Ticket Orchestrator: opencode + GPT-5.5 thinking on PO's Windows PC (skipped for TKT-017; PO ran direct dispatch)
- VPS: not engaged this cycle
- Repo path on Devin VM: `~/repos/openclown-assistant`

## 7. After self-checks pass + required reading done

Reply to the PO with:

1. **5-line state summary** in your own words (proves you read this file + the current ArchSpec + open Tickets).
2. **Role-confirmation:** "I am the Devin Orchestrator. My responsibilities include cross-TKT coordination, ratification audit on Ticket Orchestrator hand-backs (or directly on Executor + Reviewer hand-backs when the PO runs without the Ticket Orchestrator role), and final merge-safe sign-off. I will not write code, will not edit PRDs/ArchSpecs/ADRs/role prompts, and will only modify `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (lightly), and ticket frontmatter promotion fields. Other CONTRIBUTING.md edits require explicit PO authorisation, recorded in the PR body."
3. **One concrete proposed next action** with reasoning ("do X *because* Y trade-off"), citing the artifact / ADR / ticket that motivates it. The most likely concrete next action right now is: "Propose the PO dispatches Kimi K2.6 RV-SPEC review on TKT-018@0.1.0 because it is the only `draft` G-gate ticket whose review unlocks the next executor cycle in the PRD-002 G1–G4 sequence."
4. **Wait for the PO to say "go"** before executing.

## 8. If something contradicts

If this file disagrees with `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` or with `CONTRIBUTING.md` — **the repo files win, this snapshot loses**. Snapshots are volatile by definition; the repo is the source of truth across handoffs. Tell the PO what you found.
